reference_dependency_rule.description: References (terminal nodes) can be dependencies of ANY entity type
reference_dependency_rule.validation: References are always valid dependencies and bypass entity-to-entity dependency rules

reference_description.technical_architecture: Infrastructure components and system architecture elements
reference_description.platform: Platform specifications for cloud infrastructure, mobile, and web platforms
reference_description.business_logic: Detailed workflows and rules for system operations and processes
reference_description.acceptance_criterion: Success criteria for features with functional, performance, and integration requirements
reference_description.state_mutation: State change specifications for critical operations
reference_description.event: Event definitions for system actions and user interactions
reference_description.content: Complete sentences for communicating with users. Branding and taglines.
reference_description.label: Specific text labels for buttons, fields, and UI elements
reference_description.style: Specific CSS styles for UI elements and components
reference_description.color: Color values with semantic names for theming
reference_description.layout: Specific layout styles and grid configurations
reference_description.pattern: Specific UI patterns and interaction behaviors
reference_description.spacing: Spacing arrays for micro, component, and layout spacing
reference_description.typography: Font stacks, size arrays, and weight arrays
reference_description.breakpoint: Responsive design breakpoints for different screen sizes
reference_description.z-index: Z-index layers for consistent stacking of UI elements
reference_description.asset: Visual assets including images, icons, and media files for UI components and content
reference_description.api_contract: Detailed request/response schemas, error codes, rate limits for all API endpoints
reference_description.api-schema: TypeScript interface definitions for public APIs including request/response types
reference_description.validation_rule: Field-level and cross-field validation specifications for data integrity
reference_description.error_state: Error messages, recovery actions, and fallback behaviors for user-facing errors
reference_description.performance_spec: Response time SLAs, data limits, concurrency requirements, and load specifications
reference_description.notification: Alert rules, message templates, delivery channels, and notification triggers
reference_description.configuration: Feature toggles, environment configs, deployment settings, and runtime parameters
reference_description.metric: Analytics events, KPIs, success metrics, and tracking requirements for product insights
reference_description.example: Sample inputs/outputs, edge cases, test scenarios, and happy path demonstrations
reference_description.constraint: Business rules, system-wide invariants, and resource limits that must always hold
reference_description.terminology: Domain-specific terms, abbreviations, and naming conventions
reference_description.sequence: Orders of operations, initialization sequences, shutdown procedures, and event cascades
reference_description.mapping: Transformations between representations, status codes to messages, and field mappings
reference_description.default: System-wide default values, fallback behaviors, and initial states for entities
reference_description.security-spec: Reusable security requirement that may apply to multiple components
reference_description.source-file: Source code file implementations with file path and module details
reference_description.external-dep: External package dependencies from npm, pip, or other package managers
reference_description.api-surface: Public API definitions including exported functions, classes, and modules
reference_description.product-component: Cross-reference to product-level components in spec-graph.json
reference_description.signature: Function or method signature with name, parameters, and return type
reference_description.execution-trace: Step-by-step execution path through module functions with call details
reference_description.call-graph: Function call relationships showing what calls what with call types
reference_description.control-flow: Conditional logic, branches, loops, and decision points in code
reference_description.data-flow: Data transformations and how data moves through the system
reference_description.side-effect: Observable effects beyond return values: file I/O, state changes, process spawning
reference_description.error-path: Exception handling, error propagation, and recovery strategies
reference_description.code-module: Cross-reference to code-level modules in code-graph.json
reference_description.test-spec: Reusable test specification that may apply to multiple components

reference_note.purpose: References are terminal graph nodes used for template generation and detailed specifications
reference_note.schema: Each reference category uses a flat, but flexible schema tailored to its specific needs
reference_note.naming: Reference keys use plaintext naming (not key_object_notation) for final nodes
reference_note.usage: 'How' entities in the graph can reference these nodes for implementation details and specifications
reference_note.dependency_rule: References can be dependencies of ANY entity type (see reference_dependency_rule)

meta_description.workflow: Collections of operations that form complete processes
meta_description.assumption: Explicit lists of technical and business assumptions underlying the design
meta_description.phase: Project implementation phases with dependencies and parallelization information
meta_description.out_of_scope: Features and capabilities explicitly excluded from the project
meta_description.deployment: Current deployment status and rollout information for different features
meta_description.project: High-level project information including name, tagline, and brand promise
meta_description.decision: Architecture and design decisions with rationale, alternatives considered, and trade-offs
meta_description.qa_session: Ordered lists of questions to refine the project

top_level.version: STRING - Semantic version of graph format
top_level.description: STRING - Human-readable description of this knowledge graph
top_level.generated_at: ISO8601_TIMESTAMP - When this graph was generated or last updated
top_level.project_root: PATH_STRING - Absolute path to project root directory
top_level.source: PATH_STRING - Relative path to source requirements document

project.name: STRING - Full project name
project.tagline: STRING - Marketing tagline or brief pitch
project.brand_promise: STRING - Core value proposition or brand commitment
project.deployment: OBJECT<STRING:STRING|NUMBER> - Feature names mapped to deployment status or counts
project.out_of_scope: ARRAY<STRING> - Explicitly excluded features or capabilities

phase_object.name: STRING - Human-readable phase name
phase_object.parallelizable: BOOLEAN - Whether tasks in this phase can run in parallel
phase_object.requirements: ARRAY<STRING> - Entity paths that must be completed (e.g., 'requirement:system-reliability')

qa_session_object.timestamp: ISO8601_TIMESTAMP - When this Q&A session occurred
qa_session_object.questions: ARRAY<STRING> - Questions asked to refine understanding
qa_session_object.entities_added: ARRAY<STRING> - Entity paths added as result of this session
qa_session_object.notes: STRING - Optional notes or insights from session

qa_session.note: Often starts as empty array and grows during iterative refinement

decision_object.title: STRING - Decision title
decision_object.context: STRING - Why this decision was needed
decision_object.rationale: STRING - Why this choice was made
decision_object.tradeoffs: STRING - Pros and cons of the decision

workflow_object.name: STRING - Workflow name
workflow_object.operations: ARRAY<STRING> - Operation entity paths in execution order

deployment.note: Flexible structure mapping feature/component names to deployment status

out_of_scope.note: Clear list of what is NOT included in project scope

entity_description.project: Top-level container representing the entire software project or system
entity_description.requirement: Functional or non-functional specification that the system must satisfy
entity_description.interface: User interface screen or page in the application
entity_description.feature: Distinct functionality or capability provided to users
entity_description.component: Reusable building block of the system architecture
entity_description.operation: Logic and state management for component interactions
entity_description.user: Actor or role that interacts with the system
entity_description.objective: High-level goal or outcome that users want to achieve
entity_description.action: Specific user action connected to an objective

entity_note.purpose: provide containers for references and relationship structure
entity_note.schema: Entities only have name & description keys.

notes.names: reference and entity keys use snake case, use - for keys, not _
notes.schema: Do not use id, the parent key is the unique identifier
